layout_and_structure.description: Multi-column designs often cause the parser to read across the entire page, scrambling your skills with your work history.
The gold standard for 2026. Start with your most recent role and work backward for reliable machine parsing.
Most ATS cannot 'see' inside text boxes or may read table cells out of order, leading to missing data.

layout_and_structure.rule: Keep Contact Info in the Document Body
layout_and_structure.description: Do not put contact details in the 'Header' or 'Footer' sections, as many systems ignore these areas.
Stick to 'Work Experience,' 'Education,' and 'Skills' to avoid confusing the system's categorization.

visuals_and_fonts.description: Use Arial, Calibri, Helvetica, Georgia, or Times New Roman to ensure proper rendering.
Use 10–12pt for body text and 14–16pt for section headers.
Stick to standard round dots or hyphens. Fancy icons can be misinterpreted as 'garbage code'.
Avoid headshots, logos, and charts as they provide zero parseable content to the ATS.
Machines cannot read graphics; use text-based labels like 'Expert' or 'Advanced' instead.

content_and_keywords.description: Use the exact phrasing from the job posting to ensure the parser matches your skills to the requirements.
Write 'Search Engine Optimization (SEO)' at least once so the machine catches both search terms.
Use numbers and metrics (e.g., 'Increased revenue by 20%') to provide evidence-based impact.
Start bullet points with strong verbs like 'Developed,' 'Managed,' or 'Optimized'.
Place a bulleted or comma-separated list of hard skills near the top to boost keyword relevance.

technical_specifics.description: Use MM/YYYY or Month YYYY consistently to avoid timeline parsing errors.
Use .docx or text-based PDF. Avoid saving the resume as an image (JPEG/PNG).
Keep margins between 0.5 and 1.0 inch to prevent text from being cut off during conversion.
Save as 'FirstName-LastName-JobTitle.pdf' for easy identification by recruiters.
Paste your resume into a plain text editor; if the text is jumbled, the ATS will likely struggle too.
